description.value:
The user cannot be immobilized or flat-footed when performing this technique. He draws seven weapons, size category small or medium, as part of this technique and places them in peculiar locations on his body. The first two are held in the hinge joint of his right and left arms, another between his shoulder blade and neck, one in his mouth, under his left armpit, another between the hip joint of his right, raised leg and the back leg of the same raised leg.

While in this stance, the user suffers a -2 penalty to trip attempts and moves at half his base movement speed. However, the user gains a +4 circumstance bonus to his melee attack rolls and defense. In addition, during a full-attack action, the user gains two additional attacks at his highest attack bonus minus 5. He may also initiate a spin attack that provokes an attack of opportunity. This spin attack is considered a full- attack action. The user is able to attack a number of times up to 1 per 4 levels

(Maximum 3) and target multiple enemies within half his base movement speed to attack. Attacking this way incurs a -2 penalty to all attack rolls, and the user must succeed a Fortitude save (DC 20) or else be dazed for one round afterwards.

In addition to the aforementioned abilities, the user of this technique may also throw his weapons as an attack action. He may throw a number of his weapons up to his total number of attacks (as per a full attack action). Throwing weapons that are not designed to be thrown incurs a -4 penalty to all ranged attacks made in this way. If the user can reach his weapons in a single move action after attacking, he may pick them up and place them in their respective positions as a free action that does not provoke an attack of opportunity.

Requires: Balance and Tumble 12 ranks

Material Focus: Seven bladed weapons, size category Small or Medium.

Mastery: At fifth step of mastery in this technique, the user may imbue his weapons with elemental energy at the cost of 2 chakra per weapon. In addition to the weapon's normal damage, it now deals 1d6 elemental damage. The element imbued in each weapon must be the user's primary affinity.
